title: Latent-Mark: An Audio Watermark Robust to Neural Codec Compression
abstract: arXiv:2603.05310v3 Announce Type: replace-cross  Abstract: While existing audio watermarking techniques have achieved strong robustness against traditional digital signal processing (DSP) attacks, they remain vulnerable to neural compression. This occurs because modern neural audio codecs act as noise filters and discard the imperceptible waveform variations used in prior watermarking methods. To address this limitation, we propose Latent-Mark, the first zero-bit audio watermarking framework designed to survive neural codec compression. Our key insight is that robustness to the encode-decode process requires embedding the watermark within the codec's invariant latent space. We achieve this by optimizing the audio waveform to induce a detectable directional shift in its encoded latent representation, while constraining perturbations to align with the natural audio manifold to ensure imperceptibility. To prevent overfitting to a single codec's quantization rules, we introduce Cross-Codec

Title: Latent-Mark: An Audio Watermark Robust to Neural Codec Compression
Abstract: arXiv:2603.05310v3 Announce Type: replace-cross  Abstract: While existing audio watermarking techniques have achieved strong robustness against traditional digital signal processing (DSP) attacks, they remain vulnerable to neural compression. This occurs because modern neural audio codecs act as noise filters and discard the imperceptible waveform variations used in prior watermarking methods. To address this limitation, we propose Latent-Mark, the first zero-bit audio watermarking framework designed to survive neural codec compression. We achieve this by optimizing the audio waveform to induce a detectable directional shift in its encoded latent representation, while constraining perturbations to align with the natural audio manifold to ensure imperceptibility. translated_title: Latent-Mark：一种抗神经编解码器压缩的音频水印
translated_abstract: arXiv:2603.05310v3 公告类型：替换交叉 摘要：尽管现有的音频水印技术已经对传统的数字信号处理攻击展现出强大的鲁棒性，但它们仍然容易受到神经压缩的影响。这是因为现代神经音频编解码器充当了噪声滤波器，并丢弃了先前水印方法中使用的不可感知的波形变化。为了解决这一局限性，我们提出了Latent-Mark，这是第一个旨在抵抗神经编解码器压缩的零比特音频水印框架。我们的关键洞察是，要对抗编解码过程，需要将水印嵌入到编解码器的不变潜在空间中。我们通过优化音频波形，在其编码后的潜在表示中诱导出可检测的方向性偏移来实现这一点，同时约束扰动使其与自然音频流形对齐以确保不可感知性。为了防止过拟合到单一编解码器的量化规则，我们引入了跨编解码器机制。
tldr: 提出了首个抗神经编解码器压缩的零比特音频水印框架Latent-Mark，通过将水印嵌入编解码器的不变潜在空间并诱导方向性偏移，解决了现有方法易受神经压缩攻击的问题。
en_tdlr: This paper proposes Latent-Mark, the first zero-bit audio watermarking framework robust to neural codec compression, which achieves robustness by embedding watermarks in the codec's invariant latent space and inducing directional shifts.
